quality.description_clarity: The description is clear, detailing prerequisites (wind instrument proficiency), passive benefits (rats indifferent), active abilities (summoning swarms using charges), and advanced control mechanics (swaying existing swarms).
quality.mechanics_completeness: Mechanics for summoning (charges, action cost, rat availability check), passive interaction, and active control/swaying are well-defined.

charges.usage: 1 to 3 charges per action to call forth one swarm per charge, if rats are available within half a mile.

mechanics.passive_effect: Ordinary and giant rats are indifferent and will not attack unless threatened/harmed while attuned.
mechanics.active_control_summoning: Calls swarms that move toward the music but are otherwise uncontrolled.

active_control_swaying.trigger: Existing, uncontrolled swarm of rats within 30 feet while the user is playing the pipes.
active_control_swaying.resolution: Contested Charisma check vs. Swarm's Wisdom check.
active_control_swaying.success: Swayed swarm becomes friendly, obeys commands, and defends itself if commands cease. Requires continuous playing (action each round) to maintain control.
active_control_swaying.failure: Sway effect fails; swarm acts normally and cannot be swayed for 24 hours.
active_control_swaying.loss_of_control: Control ends if the swarm cannot hear the music at the start of its turn; swarm reverts and cannot be swayed for 24 hours.

mechanics.action_economy: Summoning is an Action + Bonus Action (to expend charges). Maintaining control over a swayed swarm requires the user to use an Action each round to play the pipes.

analysis.assessment: The item is mechanically rich, offering both a summoning utility and a high-risk/high-reward mechanic for gaining control over local fauna. The mechanics are detailed and internally consistent, though the requirement to continuously use an action to maintain control over a swayed swarm presents a significant opportunity cost in combat.
